epigraph.text: Ask, and it will be given to you; seek, and you will find; knock, and it will be opened to you.
epigraph.quote: The essence of mathematics lies in its freedom.

content.text:
Before developing calculus in the complex plane, we need the language of 
topology: open sets, closed sets, connectedness, and compactness. These 
concepts, while abstract, are essential for rigorous analysis.

content.title: Neighborhoods and Interior Points
content.content:
An **open disk** (or **neighborhood**) centered 
at $z_0$ with radius $r > 0$ is
\[
 D(z_0, r) = \{z \in \C : |z - z_0| < r\}.
\]
A point $z_0$ is an **interior point** of a set 
$S$ if some disk centered at $z_0$ is contained entirely in $S$.

content.title: Open and Closed Sets
content.content:
A set $S \subset \C$ is **open** if every point of $S$ 
is an interior point. A set $S$ is **closed** if its 
complement $\C \setminus S$ is open.

content.title: Open and closed sets
content.problem:
- The open disk $D(0, 1) = \{z : |z| < 1\}$ is open.

- The closed disk $\overline{D}(0, 1) = \{z : |z| \leq 1\}$ is closed.

- The punctured disk $\{z : 0 < |z| < 1\}$ is open.

- The set $\{z : |z| < 1\} \cup \{1\}$ is neither open nor closed.

- $\C$ and $\emptyset$ are both open and closed.

content.title: Boundary
content.content:
A point $z_0$ is a **boundary point** of $S$ if 
every disk centered at $z_0$ contains points in $S$ and points not in $S$. 
The set of all boundary points is the **boundary** of 
$S$, denoted $\partial S$.

content.text:
The boundary of the disk $D(0, 1)$ is the circle $\{z : |z| = 1\}$. A set 
is closed if and only if it contains all its boundary points.

content.title: Connectedness
content.content:
An open set $S$ is **connected** if any two points 
in $S$ can be joined by a polygonal path lying entirely within $S$. A 
connected open set is called a **domain** or **region**.

content.title: Connected and disconnected sets
content.problem:
- Any open disk is connected.

- The set $\{z : 1 < |z| < 2\}$ (an annulus) is connected.

- The set $\{z : |z| < 1\} \cup \{z : |z - 3| < 1\}$ is not connected.

content.text:
The concept of connectedness will be crucial when we study analytic 
continuation and the behavior of functions on domains.

content.title: Bounded Sets
content.content:
A set $S$ is **bounded** if there exists $M > 0$ 
such that $|z| \leq M$ for all $z \in S$. Equivalently, $S$ is contained 
in some disk.

content.title: Compact Sets
content.content:
A set $S \subset \C$ is **compact** if it is closed 
and bounded.

content.text:
The Heine-Borel theorem (from real analysis) extends to $\C$: a set is 
compact if and only if every open cover has a finite subcover.

content.title: Properties of Compact Sets
content.content:
Let $K \subset \C$ be compact.

1. Every sequence in $K$ has a convergent subsequence with limit in $K$.

2. If $f: K \to \C$ is continuous, then $f$ is bounded and attains its 
 maximum and minimum modulus.
content.label: Theorem

content.text: It is often useful to adjoin a "point at infinity" to the complex plane.

content.title: Riemann Sphere
content.content:
The **extended complex plane** (or 
**Riemann sphere**) is 
$\hat{\C} = \C \cup \{\infty\}$, where $\infty$ is a single point 
representing "infinity in all directions."

content.text:
The Riemann sphere can be visualized via stereographic projection: place a 
unit sphere on the complex plane with its south pole at the origin. For each 
point $z \in \C$, draw a line from the north pole through $z$; it intersects 
the sphere at a unique point. The north pole itself corresponds to $\infty$.

Under this identification, the Riemann sphere is topologically a sphere, 
and "going to infinity" becomes approaching the north pole.

marginNotes.text: A threefold cord is not quickly broken.

marginNotes.author: Felix Hausdorff
marginNotes.text:
A set is a Many that allows itself to be 
thought of as a One.
